title: catha europaea
intro: Catha europaea, also known as khat, is a flowering plant native to the Horn of Africa and the Arabian Peninsula, belonging to the Celastraceae family. The plant contains cathinone, a stimulant that releases dopamine and norepinephrine in the brain, providing a sense of euphoria and alertness. Traditionally, khat has been used for its medicinal properties, including treating fatigue, anxiety, and depression, as well as improving cognitive function and memory. Various herbal preparations, such as dried leaves or extracts, are available, but its use is often associated with risks, including addiction and cardiovascular problems.
